title: Samantha Power Returns
summary: Samantha Power, United States Ambassador to the United Nations from 2013 until 2017, has been named to a joint faculty appointment at Harvard Kennedy School and Harvard Law School.
body: Will Head New International Peace and Security Project The latest from Belfer See more Samantha Power, United States Ambassador to the United Nations from 2013 until 2017, has been named to a joint faculty appointment at Harvard Kennedy School and Harvard Law School. At the Kennedy School, she will direct a new Belfer Center initiative—the International Peace and Security Project—and will be a senior member of the Center and member of the board. She will also be affiliated with the Kennedy School’s Carr Center for Human Rights Policy. Power first came to the Belfer Center in 1998 as Project Director of the Human Rights Initiative. That initiative grew quickly and became the Carr Center, where she was named the founding Executive Director. On her return to the Kennedy School, Power will serve as the Anna Lindh Professor of the Practice of Global Leadership and Public Policy. At Harvard Law School, where she earned her J.D. in 1999, she will be Professor of Practice. Prior to becoming UN Ambassador, Power served for four years as President Obama’s Special Assistant for Multilateral Affairs and Human Rights on the National Security Council. At the UN, she negotiated the toughest sanctions in a generation against North Korea, became the public face of U.S. opposition to Russian actions in Ukraine and Syria, lobbied to help secure the release of political prisoners around the world, and spearheaded UN reforms, including of peacekeeping efforts. Before her academic career, Power served as a foreign correspondent, contributing to The New Yorker, New Republic, and New York Review of Books. Her books include the 2003 Pulitzer Prize winning A Problem from Hell: America and the Age of Genocide, which examined U.S. responses to genocide in the 20th century. “We could not be prouder to welcome Samantha home,” said Belfer Center Director Graham Allison. “She has been a source of inspiration and insight, and her extensive service both in Washington and as Ambassador to the United Nations will inform and enliven the intellectual life at the Center.” Former Secretary of Defense Ash Carter , who will succeed Allison as Belfer Center Director in July, said, “Few have championed human rights and international law with the rigor and real-world accomplishments of Sam Power. She is a huge asset to the Belfer Center.” “I am very excited to return to Harvard, as I believe it is essential that we do all we can to ensure that graduates have the skills they need to succeed in messy geopolitical and multilateral environments,” Power said. “Given the daunting challenges we confront—whether from terrorism, rising nationalism, climate change, or mass atrocities—it is essential that we in academia draw lessons from experience, devise practical approaches, and prepare the next generation to improve their communities, their countries, and the world.” Recommended citation "Samantha Power Returns." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School (Summer 2017). result.main_content_en: Samantha Power, former U.S. Ambassador to the UN, has been appointed to a joint faculty position at Harvard Kennedy School and Harvard Law School. At the Kennedy School, she will direct the Belfer Center's new International Peace and Security Project, serve as a senior member and board member of the Center, and be affiliated with the Carr Center for Human Rights Policy. She will hold the Anna Lindh Professor of the Practice of Global Leadership and Public Policy title. At Harvard Law School, she will be a Professor of Practice. Power's extensive background includes serving as President Obama's Special Assistant for Multilateral Affairs and Human Rights, negotiating sanctions against North Korea, leading U.S. opposition to Russian actions in Ukraine and Syria, advocating for political prisoners, and spearheading UN reforms. Before her government service, she was a foreign correspondent and Pulitzer Prize-winning author. Power stated her goal is to equip graduates with skills for complex geopolitical and multilateral challenges, drawing from experience to prepare the next generation.
result.main_content_zh: 美国前常驻联合国代表萨曼莎·鲍尔被任命为哈佛大学肯尼迪政府学院和哈佛法学院的联合教职。在肯尼迪学院，她将领导贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心新设立的“国际和平与安全项目”，担任该中心的高级成员和董事会成员，并与人权政策卡尔中心建立联系。她将担任安娜·林德全球领导力与公共政策实践教授。在哈佛法学院，她将担任实践教授。鲍尔拥有丰富的履历，曾担任奥巴马总统的多边事务和人权特别助理，主导了对朝鲜的严厉制裁，是美国在乌克兰和叙利亚问题上反对俄罗斯行动的公开代表，推动释放全球政治犯，并领导了联合国维和等改革。在从政前，她曾是驻外记者和普利策奖获奖作家。鲍尔表示，她的目标是让毕业生掌握应对复杂地缘政治和多边环境所需的技能，从经验中汲取教训，为下一代应对恐怖主义、民族主义抬头、气候变化或大规模暴行等挑战做好准备。
keywords_en: Carr Center for Human Rights Policy
